title: Geospatial — Fabric maps
description: Intent-scoped fabio skill for Fabric geospatial maps: create, inspect, and update map items and their definitions. Use to manage location/geospatial visualizations backed by Fabric data. Triggers: "map", "geospatial", "create map", "location visualization", "spatial data", "map definition".
when_to_use: Creating/listing/inspecting map items in a workspace.
Managing geospatial visualizations over Fabric data.
when_not_to_use: The underlying spatial data (tables/files) -> use fabio-lakehouse or fabio-warehouse-sql.
Non-spatial reports and dashboards -> use fabio-bi.
must: Edit map content via the definition part (get-definition / update-definition), not plain metadata flags.
Ensure the spatial data source the map reads from exists before wiring the map.
prefer: Runtime introspection (context agent --group map, context describe map create) for exact flags and the definition shape.
Building the spatial dataset first (lakehouse/warehouse), then the map on top.
avoid: Expecting a metadata-only update to change map content — use update-definition.
Confusing a map (geospatial visualization) with a graph model or an ontology.
key_gotchas: Maps carry their configuration in a definition part (base64); create/update take a --content/definition, and get-definition returns it for round-tripping.
A map is a visualization layer — it renders data that lives in other Fabric items, which must exist and be populated.

troubleshooting.fix: Update the definition (map update-definition), not just metadata; metadata-only updates don't change map content.
Confirm the backing spatial dataset exists and is populated; the map only visualizes other items' data.

safety: Deleting a map removes its definition and configuration — confirm with the user.
